title: Bosnia and Herzegovina - Country Partnership Framework : Chair Summary
text:
﻿                                           Bosnia and Herzegovina

                              Country Partnership Framework FY23-27

                                                July 19, 2022

                                              Chair Summary*


The Executive Directors discussed the World Bank Group Country Partnership Framework (CPF) for
Bosnia and Herzegovina (BiH) for FY23-27. Directors appreciated the focus of the CPF Higher-Level
Outcomes (HLOs) and welcomed the alignment of the CPF with the Green, Resilient, and Inclusive
Development (GRID) approach and the Systematic Country Diagnostic. Directors also appreciated
the integration of gender as a cross-cutting issue and asked to put in place adequate reporting on gender
measures to ensure results on the ground and closing of the gender gap.

While acknowledging the complex environment, Directors noted the progress achieved during the
previous CPF cycle and welcomed the flexibility built into the program to ensure sound fiscal and
macro policies, strengthen institutional capacity, and deliver a greener and more inclusive development
path, emphasizing the need to build on the WBG’s comparative advantage and lessons learned from
the previous CPF. They stressed the need for additional risk mitigation measures to safeguard
implementation against uncertainties associated with governance risks.

Directors stressed the need to support private sector development and improved competitiveness to
spur growth, particularly in the context of the lower fiscal resources, and to mitigate risks that might
hamper investments. They urged the WBG to continue supporting government’s reform efforts through
initiatives that nurture a conducive investment climate. They highlighted the importance of improving
labor market flexibility with a focus on increasing labor force participation of women and youth.

Directors noted that the country’s Gross National Income per capita is nearing the IBRD Graduation
Discussion Income and encouraged continued attention to strengthening of institutions that are key for
delivering on the Higher-Level Outcomes. They emphasized the need for continued WBG engagement
to help strengthen governance and citizen participation to increase accountability and public sector
performance. Directors urged Bosnia and Herzegovina to accelerate reforms related to the green
agenda and public service delivery and competitiveness.

Directors commended the One WBG approach described by the CPF and encouraged IFC and MIGA
to seek opportunities to enhance the country’s productivity and foster the creation of more and better
private sector jobs. They stressed the need for continued close collaboration with other development
partners during implementation of the program.




_____________________________
*This Summary is not an approved record.
